project_title: Book Trilogy - Security, Dialectic, Repair : a three-book governed arc on dangerous power, democratic formation, and political repair after harm (the full manuscript corpus across 72 chapters)
runtime_target: chapter dossier system with argument, source, title, voice, visual, and handoff gates
operational_end_state: Each of 72 chapters carries a public title, argument backbone, source burden, book-specific voice rule, visual identity, revision status, and handoff to adjacent chapters.
full_build_definition: A manuscript system with chapter or section structure, source-governed argument logic, revision state, passage targets, reader-facing output, and publication-readiness checks.
minimum_operational_release: A chapter or manuscript release slice for Book Trilogy Manuscript Governance System: outline, argument state, source/passages used, revision rules, public title logic, and next reader-facing output.
required_modules_to_build: chapter dossier registry | argument backbone field | source state and close-read queue | voice/register constitution | visual-title governance | cross-chapter handoff | revision status
source_status: 7 source-application rows; 0 active passage contracts; 0 unlocated source candidates.
source_actions_before_claim: Carry 7 source-application role(s) into the first build release and convert the most load-bearing source into a close-read contract before any quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim.
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | state-transition test: visible input changes recorded state and output | chapter uniqueness test: title, visual, source, and argument are chapter-specific
acceptance_tests: Inspect outline, source/passages, argument movement, revision status, public title logic, and continuity with the declared book or publication role.
promotion_stage: full-build candidate; execute minimum operational release next
current_status: specified proof plan; needs live or replayable demonstrator
launch_or_review_path: Open the manuscript/project contract for `trilogy`, select one chapter or section, and produce a source-bound release slice.
first_full_build: Build one complete representative dossier per book and then scale the same fields across all 72 chapters.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
public_claim_now: May claim a full-build candidate exists with proof and source contracts; do not claim deployment, outside validation, or production readiness until live execution and passage verification are recorded.
blocking_gates: live capture and outside-use evidence before deployment or validation claims
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
